name: deep-plan
purpose: Structured discovery-first planning that front-loads decisions through exhaustive questioning, producing DIAGNOSIS.md, DECISIONS.md, and PLAN.md artifacts with convergence-loop verification before any code is written.
sanitize_fields: external_refs[0] (write-invocation.ts is SoNash-specific; JASON-OS uses a no-op stub or omits)
notes: SoNash version 3.3 (2026-03-15). File count: 2. Size: 28324 bytes. Companion files: REFERENCE.md (flat layout). JASON-OS has a ported version at .claude/skills/deep-plan/ with version 3.0 per agentskills-v1 metadata. JASON-OS port drops invocation tracking step (write-invocation.ts absent). SoNash version references gsd:plan-phase, gsd:new-project (GSD not in JASON-OS), research-plan-team (.claude/teams/). Phase 0 extracts from .research/EXTRACTIONS.md and .research/extraction-journal.jsonl (SoNash-specific; absent in JASON-OS). VERSION DELTA: SoNash 3.3 vs JASON-OS 3.0 — missing 3.1 (code-state verification), 3.2 (CL integration Phase 0+3.5), 3.3 (skill-audit: failure paths, Integration section). These are behavioral improvements, no scripts added. Invocation tracking uses write-invocation.ts in SoNash but is omitted in JASON-OS port.

name: webapp-testing
purpose: Playwright-based toolkit for testing local web applications, supporting DOM inspection, screenshot capture, browser log capture, and multi-server lifecycle management via Python helper scripts.
notes: Version 1.0 (2026-02-25). File count: 5. Size: 23300 bytes. Companion files: scripts/with_server.py, examples/element_discovery.py, examples/static_html_automation.py, examples/console_logging.py, LICENSE.txt. Python-based skill (only non-Node skill in this tier). Methodology: black-box script invocation with --help first, do not read source. Decision tree: static HTML -> read selectors directly; dynamic -> reconnaissance-then-action pattern. LICENSE.txt is unusual — no other skills in tiers D1a-D1d have a separate LICENSE.txt. Portability is high — no SoNash-specific references; framework-agnostic Playwright scripts. JASON-OS status: not ported (Python infra not standard in JASON-OS v0).

name: code-reviewer
purpose: Ad-hoc code review toolkit for SoNash (Next.js/TypeScript/Firebase) covering anti-pattern detection, security checklist, framework-specific patterns, and episodic memory search for past review context.
sanitize_fields: Review checklist: Firebase/Firestore section (httpsCallable, Firestore collections journal/daily_logs/inventoryEntries — SoNash product-specific)
React/Next.js section references App Router conventions (SoNash stack)
docs/agent_docs/ references (CODE_PATTERNS.md, POSITIVE_PATTERNS.md etc — SoNash-specific docs)
notes: Version 2.2 (2026-03-13). File count: 7. Size: 23243 bytes. Companion files: references/code_review_checklist.md, references/coding_standards.md, references/common_antipatterns.md, scripts/code_quality_checker.py, scripts/pr_analyzer.py, scripts/review_report_generator.py. reference_layout: subdirectory (references/ subdir). JASON-OS has a ported skill at .claude/skills/code-reviewer/ but it is SKILL.md-only (no companion scripts or references dir). Episodic memory MCP is SoNash-specific; JASON-OS uses different memory approach. Script checklist (items 10-11 about FIX_TEMPLATES.md and SECURITY_CHECKLIST.md) references SoNash docs/ structure. Anti-pattern verification in §D27/D32 references patterns counting from 259 SoNash reviews — not portable directly.

name: ux-researcher-designer
purpose: UX research and design toolkit providing data-driven persona generation, journey mapping, usability testing frameworks, and research synthesis for user-centered design work.
sanitize_fields: scope field — scoped to project (SoNash UX context); remove SoNash-specific references if porting
notes: Version 1.0 (2026-02-25). File count: 2. Size: 22795 bytes. Companion files: scripts/persona_generator.py. SKILL.md is notably thin — mostly a Python script wrapper with minimal skill protocol (no phases, no guard rails, no invocation tracking, no version-gated MUST/SHOULD hierarchy). Portability is high in principle but scope is project because persona generation is tailored to SoNash's user base. Python-based companion script. JASON-OS status: not ported. Scope set to project because without SoNash user data, persona generation lacks grounding.

name: brainstorm
purpose: Creative discovery phase using Socratic dialogue, research-grade agents, and contrarian challenge to move from seed ideas to chosen direction with a BRAINSTORM.md artifact feeding downstream /deep-plan or /deep-research.
notes: Version 1.1 (2026-04-01). File count: 2. Size: 20326 bytes. Companion files: REFERENCE.md (flat layout). JASON-OS has a ported version at .claude/skills/brainstorm/ with version 1.0 per agentskills-v1 metadata — VERSION DELTA: SoNash is 1.1 (2026-04-01 skill-audit adding phase gates, CL integration, guard rails, UX) while JASON-OS is 1.0. The 1.1 improvements are behavioral-only (no scripts added), so the gap is low-risk but meaningful for skill completeness. References sc:brainstorm as external plugin with different methodology — this distinction is portable. Phase 0 step 3 references .research/EXTRACTIONS.md which is SoNash-specific (not in JASON-OS v0).

name: ui-design-system

sanitize_fields: scope — SoNash project-scoped design tokens; brand color inputs must be replaced for any port
design_token_generator.py outputs CSS/SCSS/JSON — downstream consumers are SoNash build system
notes: Version 1.0 (2026-02-25). File count: 2. Size: 19747 bytes. Companion files: scripts/design_token_generator.py. Python-based script, no SKILL.md phases or protocol sections (thin skill structure — no phases, guard rails, compaction resilience, or invocation tracking). Same structural thinness as ux-researcher-designer — these two appear to be early-bootstrap skills from the same creation batch. Portability is conceptually high but scope is project because design tokens are brand-specific. JASON-OS status: not ported.

name: todo
purpose: Cross-session todo management with 8-option interactive menu, JSONL-backed persistence via locked regression-guarded CLI, and AI-powered context capture — independent of GSD.
sanitize_fields: external_refs[0] (write-invocation.ts — SoNash-specific; JASON-OS omits or stubs)
active_scripts (scripts/planning/todos-cli.js and render-todos.js are SoNash scripts; JASON-OS has equivalent scripts but path structure may differ)
Session-start hook integration (SoNash-specific hook output format)
session-end integration (reads todos.jsonl — path portable but SoNash session-end structure differs from JASON-OS)
notes: Version 1.2 (2026-04-10). File count: 2. Size: 19113 bytes. Companion files: REFERENCE.md (flat layout). JASON-OS has a ported version at .claude/skills/todo/ with version 1.2 per agentskills-v1 metadata — VERSIONS MATCH. T30 fix (locked regression-guarded CLI) is the defining feature of 1.2: all JSONL mutations must go through scripts/planning/todos-cli.js with advisory lock + regression guard. JASON-OS has equivalent CLI scripts. Invocation tracking via write-invocation.ts is SoNash-specific and stripped in JASON-OS port. Storage paths (.planning/todos.jsonl, .planning/TODOS.md) are portable conventions.

name: audit-ai-optimization
purpose: 11-agent 3-stage parallel AI optimization audit covering 12 domains: dead assets, fragile parsing, format waste, AI instruction bloat, hook latency, skill overlap, agent prompt quality, MCP config efficiency, context optimization, and memory/state management.
notes: Version 1.1 (2026-02-23). File count: 1 (SKILL.md only). Size: 18948 bytes. No companion files. Unique frontmatter fields: supports_parallel:true, fallback_available:true, estimated_time_parallel/sequential. 11 agents across 3 stages — uses Task tool subagent_type 'Explore' and 'general-purpose'. Delegates shared protocol to _shared/AUDIT_TEMPLATE.md (reads inline from SoNash). JASON-OS status: not ported. Portability requires: (1) stripping TDMS intake step, (2) replacing _shared/AUDIT_TEMPLATE.md reference, (3) adapting output paths, (4) replacing episodic-memory MCP call. Core 12-domain analysis logic is broadly applicable to any Claude Code project. The 'AI instruction bloat' domain directly addresses SKILL.md line counts — relevant to JASON-OS itself.

name: session-end
purpose: 10-step session closure pipeline covering context preservation, compliance review, metrics pipeline (TDMS/reviews/ecosystem-health), state cleanup, pre-commit summary, and final commit/push.
sanitize_fields: Phase 3 TDMS pipeline (consolidate-all.js, generate-metrics.js, MASTER_DEBT.jsonl — SoNash TDMS-specific)
Phase 3 reviews:sync (v1 bridge — SoNash-specific reviews pipeline)
Step 4b agent-invocations.jsonl (Layer 2 hook output — gated in JASON-OS)
npm run session:end (SoNash npm script; JASON-OS has node scripts/session-end-commit.js)
notes: SoNash version 2.2 (2026-03-13). File count: 1 (SKILL.md only). Size: 18365 bytes. JASON-OS has ported version at .claude/skills/session-end/ with version 2.2-jasonos-v0.1 (2026-04-17). JASON-OS PORT SCOPE: Phase 1 (Steps 1-3) + Phase 4 (Steps 8-10) are LIVE. Phase 2 (Steps 4-6) annotated as Layer-2-gated (mostly no-op until Layer 2 hooks land). Phase 3 (metrics pipeline: reviews:sync, ecosystem-health, TDMS) is STRIPPED entirely — requires SoNash infrastructure not in JASON-OS Foundation. SESSION_HISTORY.md archival replaced with simpler 5-field SESSION_CONTEXT.md updates per D12. `npm run session:end` replaced with `node scripts/session-end-commit.js`. orphaned synthesize.state.json check is retained (portable). VERSION DELTA: Phase 3 is the major missing piece in JASON-OS; Steps 4b/4c/5b/7g (D26 data flow) are also absent.
deferred_sections: Phase 2 Steps 4/4b/4c/5/5b/6 (Layer 2 hook outputs not yet wired)
Phase 3 all metrics scripts (TDMS/reviews/ecosystem-health — SoNash infrastructure not in JASON-OS Foundation)
Step 7g commit analytics (commit-log.jsonl — Layer 2 hook output)
port_lineage_frontmatter: Lineage: SoNash 41526 `session-end` v2.2 → JASON-OS v0.1 port

name: debt-runner
purpose: 7-mode interactive TDMS orchestrator (verify/sync/plan/health/dedup/validate/cleanup) with convergence-loop verification at every stage, staging-file safety, and remediation plan artifact ownership.
notes: Version 1.1 (2026-03-15). File count: 2. Size: 18260 bytes. Companion files: REFERENCE.md (flat layout). Portability: not-portable because every mode hardcodes SoNash TDMS paths: docs/technical-debt/MASTER_DEBT.jsonl (4500+ items), docs/technical-debt/staging/, docs/technical-debt/raw/deduped.jsonl, and calls 37 TDMS scripts (scripts/debt/). JASON-OS has `add-debt` as a stub (per Piece 1a §5 and MEMORY.md) but NOT debt-runner. The MEMORY.md TDMS upgrade note says debt-runner is the TDMS upgrade target for JASON-OS's add-debt stub. PORTING STRATEGY: debt-runner cannot be ported without first porting the TDMS infrastructure (MASTER_DEBT.jsonl + scripts/debt/* pipeline). Once TDMS lands in JASON-OS, debt-runner becomes sanitize-then-portable with REFERENCE.md as the menu/schema template.

name: audit-enhancements
purpose: 4-phase adaptive enhancement audit deploying 8+ parallel agents across product/code/UX/DevX/infrastructure/testing/docs/workflow domains, producing TDMS-ingested findings with mandatory counter-arguments and a no-change strengths section.
external_refs: SKIP_AUDIT_VALIDATION=1 git commit (pre-commit compat escape hatch)
sanitize_fields: Agent 2 Product/UX domain: recovery-domain sensitivity flag (SoNash product-specific therapeutic content warning)
Agent 3 Content domain: recovery-domain terminology appropriateness (SoNash product context)
Agent 5 Infrastructure: Firebase config, Next.js config, firebase.json references (SoNash stack-specific)
SKIP_AUDIT_VALIDATION=1 escape hatch (SoNash-specific pre-commit schema check)
notes: Version 1.2 (2026-02-23). File count: 1 (SKILL.md only). Size: 17871 bytes. No companion files. Unique frontmatter fields: no supports_parallel/fallback_available (unlike other audit-* skills). Phase 1 uses CRITICAL distinction: subagent_type 'general-purpose' NOT 'Explore' (Explore is read-only, cannot write JSONL). Phase 2 deep-dive is opt-in after Phase 1 results. Honesty requirement (counter_argument REQUIRED for every finding) is a key differentiator. Severity calibration anchor table uses SoNash-specific examples but the pattern is portable. Recovery-domain sensitivity flags are SoNash product-specific and must be removed on port. JASON-OS status: not ported.

name: audit-documentation
purpose: 18-agent 6-stage parallel documentation audit covering inventory, link validation, content quality, format compliance, placement/lifecycle analysis, and synthesis — with TDMS intake and interactive review.
sanitize_fields: prompts.md companion file (extracted from SKILL.md at v2.2 — all agent prompts, JSONL schemas, report templates live there)
notes: Version 2.2 (2026-02-24). File count: 2 (SKILL.md + prompts.md). Size: 16531 bytes. Companion files: prompts.md (all agent prompt text, JSONL schemas, report templates extracted at v2.2 to keep SKILL.md under 500 lines). Delegates ALL agent prompts to prompts.md — SKILL.md is orchestration-only. 18 agents across 6 stages. Stage 2 agent 2B (external URL checker) is interesting: external URL validation is broadly useful for any project. Context recovery state file keyed by audit date. JASON-OS status: not ported. Portability requires: prompts.md must come with SKILL.md, TDMS intake steps removed/replaced, documentation standards reference adapted.

name: developer-growth-analysis
purpose: Analyzes Claude Code chat history (~/.claude/history.jsonl) for development patterns and growth areas, curates HackerNews learning resources, and delivers a personalized growth report to Slack DMs via Rube MCP.
external_refs: RUBE_MULTI_EXECUTE_TOOL (Slack DM delivery via Rube MCP)
notes: Version 1.0 (2026-02-25). File count: 1 (SKILL.md only). Size: 16357 bytes. No companion files. Portability: not-portable because it depends on Rube MCP (proprietary external service) for both HackerNews search and Slack DM delivery. Rube MCP is not in JASON-OS and cannot be assumed present. Without Rube MCP, Step 5 (HackerNews curation) and Step 7 (Slack delivery) would need replacement (e.g., use WebSearch for HackerNews, omit Slack delivery or replace with file output). SKILL.md is thin on protocol (no phases, guard rails, compaction resilience, invocation tracking). The chat history read from ~/.claude/history.jsonl is a user-home path — cross-locale concern but technically portable if that file exists. Scope set to 'user' because it reads personal coding history. JASON-OS status: not ported. Rube MCP dependency is the blocking factor.

name: audit-aggregator
purpose: Synthesizes findings from all 9 domain audit reports into a single deduplicated priority-ranked comprehensive report with cross-domain pattern detection, TDMS intake, and roadmap integration.
sanitize_fields: All 5 TDMS post-audit steps (requires SoNash debt pipeline)
9 expected domain audit report filenames (SoNash-specific audit suite)
examples.md companion file (deduplication code and merge examples)
notes: Version 1.6 (2026-02-24). File count: 2 (SKILL.md + examples.md). Size: 16150 bytes. Companion files: examples.md (deduplication code, merge examples, formula weights, track assignment rules). SKILL.md is orchestration-only; all code examples extracted to examples.md at v1.6. Depends on 9 domain-specific audit reports existing in docs/audits/comprehensive/audit-YYYY-MM-DD/ — makes it a downstream consumer of the full audit-comprehensive pipeline. The dedup/cross-domain pattern detection logic is broadly reusable. JASON-OS status: not ported. Portability requires: stripping TDMS 5-step checklist, replacing audit domain list with target-project's audit family, adapting output paths.

name: session-begin
purpose: Pre-flight checklist for work sessions — loads context, increments session counter, runs 8 health scripts, gates on warning acknowledgment, and presents goal selection before work begins.
sanitize_fields: Phase 1.2 SessionStart hook cross-session validation (hook behavior differs between SoNash/JASON-OS)
Phase 2.4 npm run session:gaps (SoNash npm script)
Phase 3 all 8 health scripts (all npm run scripts are SoNash-specific)
Phase 4.1 health-score-log.jsonl drop detection (SoNash state file)
Phase 4.2 hook-warnings.json gate (partially portable — JASON-OS has hook-warnings-log.jsonl)
notes: SoNash version 2.0 (2026-03-16). File count: 2. Size: 14223 bytes. Companion files: REFERENCE.md (flat layout). JASON-OS has ported version at .claude/skills/session-begin/ with version 2.1 (2026-04-17). VERSION NOTE: JASON-OS is version 2.1 which is HIGHER than SoNash's 2.0 — JASON-OS received bootstrap-specific changes (DEFERRED markers, trimming infrastructure references) that incremented the version. JASON-OS DEFERRED SECTIONS: Phase 1.1 (secrets decryption), Phase 1.2 (cross-session validation), Phase 2.4-2.6 (session:gaps, consolidation, research-index), Phase 3 entire (all 8 health scripts), Phase 3.1-3.2 (crossdoc/task-deps), Phase 4.1 override/health-score trend checks, Phase 4.2 warning gate (retained but data may be absent), Phase 4.3 session-start failures + test registry, Phase 4.4 tech-debt snapshot. LIVE in JASON-OS: duplicate detection, session counter increment, SESSION_CONTEXT.md load, branch validation, stale docs check, hook-warnings-log check, goal selection, closure signal. Velocity references (scripts/velocity/) removed in commit 7e74fada — this is a SoNash-side annotation in the skill itself, indicating a past cleanup.

name: audit-security
purpose: 12-category 4-agent parallel security audit (or sequential fallback) covering auth/authorization, input validation, data protection, Firebase/Firestore, dependency supply chain, OWASP, hosting headers, Next.js framework, file handling, crypto, product/UX security, and AI-generated code risks.
sanitize_fields: Agent 2 supply-chain-auditor: functions/package.json reference (SoNash-specific Firebase Functions)
Agent 3 framework-security-auditor: firebase.json, firestore.rules, storage.rules, next.config.mjs, functions/src/ (SoNash stack)
Scope include/exclude lists referencing SoNash dirs (app/, components/, lib/, functions/)
notes: Version 1.0 (2026-02-25). File count: 1 (SKILL.md only). Size: 13223 bytes. No companion files. Parallel mode: 4 agents (vulnerability-scanner, supply-chain-auditor, framework-security-auditor, ai-code-security-auditor). Each agent is scoped to SoNash stack files — agents 2-4 are deeply SoNash-specific (Firebase, Next.js, functions/). Agent 1 (auth/input validation) and Agent 4 (AI-generated code/agent security) are the most portable. Domain 12/13 'AI Security Patterns' is notable: specifically targets .claude/ configs for prompt-injection surfaces — universally applicable to any Claude Code project. JASON-OS status: not ported. Core security methodology is solid but agents need complete redefinition for non-Firebase/non-Next.js targets.

name: audit-refactoring
purpose: 3-agent parallel refactoring audit covering god objects, code duplication, cognitive complexity (SonarCloud CRITICAL targets), architecture violations (circular deps), and technical debt markers — with sequential fallback.
sanitize_fields: Agent 1 file scope: app/**/*.tsx, components/**/*.tsx (SoNash Next.js dirs)
Agent 2 SonarCloud CRITICAL cognitive complexity: baseline references '47 CRITICAL as of 2026-01-05' (SoNash-specific metric)
npm run deps:circular / deps:unused (SoNash npm scripts)
AUDIT_TRACKER.md thresholds (40+ commits, 3+ complexity warnings — SoNash calibration)
notes: Version 1.0 (2026-02-25). File count: 1 (SKILL.md only). Size: 12398 bytes. No companion files. Parallel mode: 3 agents (structure-and-duplication, complexity-and-coupling, dead-code-and-markers). Agent 2 includes a SonarCloud CRITICAL target hardcoded to SoNash baseline (47 CRITICAL issues). Step 6 check template currency references REFACTORING_AUDIT.md with SoNash-specific baselines (778 issues, 47 CRITICAL). Core logic (god objects >300 lines, functions >50 lines, circular deps) is framework-agnostic and broadly applicable. JASON-OS status: not ported. The god-object threshold and complexity checks would directly apply to JASON-OS's scripts/ infra.

name: data-effectiveness-audit
purpose: 8-phase systematic audit of all data systems using lifecycle scoring (Capture/Storage/Recall/Action, 0-3 each, 0-12 total) to identify write-only data, broken feedback loops, and enforcement gaps, routing gaps through the learning-to-automation pipeline.
sanitize_fields: .claude/state/lifecycle-scores.jsonl (SoNash-specific state file — would need to be bootstrapped in new project)
notes: Version 1.0 (2026-03-13). File count: 1 (SKILL.md only). Size: 12259 bytes. No companion files. The lifecycle scoring framework (C/S/R/A, 0-3 per dimension, A-F grades) is the most portable element — the 4-dimensional scoring model is broadly applicable to any project's data systems. The 8 audit domains are also conceptually portable. However, the execution pipeline (6 scripts, lifecycle-scores.jsonl state, .planning/system-wide-standardization/ artifact path) is all SoNash-specific and requires scaffolding before use in a new project. Critical Rule 1 (read lifecycle-scores.jsonl first) means this skill has no entry point without prior bootstrapping. Routing to /comprehensive-ecosystem-audit and /health-ecosystem-audit links it to the broader SoNash ecosystem audit family. Phase 5 routes gaps through learning-to-automation pipeline — this pipeline is entirely SoNash-specific. JASON-OS status: not ported.
